weather_report: Late morning in Subiaco arrives under a polished sky, the sunlight already clean and insistent, sharpening edges on verandas and gum leaves. The forecast holds clear through midday into early afternoon, promising a bright, transparent day with that familiar pavement glare. Even in autumn the UV will climb to a moderate bite around lunch, so a touch of sunscreen, a brimmed hat, and sunglasses are wise if you linger. Dress in light layers you can unbutton as the sun gathers. It’s the kind of calm, well-lit day made for ordinary pleasures: hang the washing, walk an errand instead of driving, claim a table on a cafe terrace, or pause on a park bench with a book while the warmth finds your sleeves. If you’re working, crack a window and let the steady light keep you company; save a short amble just after noon when the clarity is at its most persuasive.
